name: The heart of the Garraf park in gravel
name_original: El corazón del parque del Garraf en gravel
description:
This Route takes you into the heart of the Garraf Park, a space of natural interest located in the massif of the same name. Due to its proximity to the sea and geological nature dominated by limestone, this space stands out for its landscape and karst modeling, full of caves and balmas and a typically Mediterranean climate. The human presence inside the park and the massif comes from the cultivation of vineyards and corn. The route runs mainly through sparsely populated areas, but along the Route you find vestiges of human activity in the form of dry stone cabins and lime kilns.

The tour begins in the town of Sant Pere de Ribes. After crossing the town you head towards Can Coll after crossing the bridge of the same name that spans the Ribes stream. Can Coll is a monumental farmhouse that stands out for its two-story gallery with twenty-three semicircular arch porticos. From there you pedal between the fields that separate this town from Sitges, towards the Garraf massif. The climb to the massif is done along the so-called Sitges track. To get to it, and avoid roads or access through urbanizations in the area, I have chosen a path that has an uphill section of about 300 meters in which there are several points with a lot of stones. You may have to push the bike.

The climb along the Sitges track is not very long. It is about two and a half kilometers with some hard sections, over 10%, but the road is in very good condition, and is even partially paved. When the slope stabilizes, you have a seven-kilometer stretch ahead of you, much gentler, with only the occasional short, hard slope. After crossing the road that goes up to Rat Penat, you enter the Villagrassa trail, a delight to do on a gravel bike. Then you enter the paved road that leads to Olivella. Here you will find the hardest ramps to reach the Garraf astronomical observatory, from where you continue along the asphalt to the charming and picturesque town of Olivella. In Olivella there is a “food truck” open from Wednesday to Sunday, much appreciated by cyclists and hikers for its sandwiches, cookies and cakes.

The return towards Sant Pere de Ribes follows a path parallel to the road, until in the Mas Milà urbanization, you enter the track that follows the bottom of the Querol. After almost two kilometers, you enter a narrow path with a gentle climb surrounded by pine trees and ferns. You find some stones, but they are the exception, it is a very beautiful and pleasant part of the Route! When leaving the path comes the descent, through the streets of the Can Pere de la Plana urbanization. Before returning to the starting point you cross a small forest where you ride along a path where roots and stones abound, but it is very cyclable and has enough flow, even for a gravel bike.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Sant Pere de Ribes offers routes through the diverse landscapes of the Garraf region in Catalonia, Spain. The area is characterized by the rugged limestone formations and pine forests of the Garraf Natural Park, alongside rolling hills and vineyards. Seasonal riverbeds, known as "rieras," carve through the terrain, providing varied natural corridors for exploration. This blend of natural features creates a dynamic environment for gravel cyclists. Best gravel bike trails around Sant Pere de Ribes The most popular gravel bike trail is The heart of the Garraf park in gravel, a 23.1 miles (37.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the varied terrain of the Garraf Natural Park, featuring limestone formations and pine forests. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Climb to the Garraf viewpoint on gravel, a difficult 25.8 miles (41.5 km) path. This route ascends to a Garraf viewpoint, offering panoramic views over the rugged peaks and valleys of the natural park. Local gravel bikers also love the On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf, a 34.6 miles (55.6 km) trail leading through coastal fringes and the Garraf Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 17 minutes. Gravel biking around Sant Pere de Ribes is defined by limestone formations, pine forests, and vineyards. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging climbs within the natural park to gentler routes through agricultural plains. The routes in Sant Pere de Ribes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1000 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Pere de Ribes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available around Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: There are over 160 gravel bike routes to explore around Sant Pere de Ribes, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll encounter rugged limestone formations and pine forests within the Garraf Natural Park, alongside rolling hills and picturesque vineyards. Some routes also feature seasonal riverbeds, known as 'rieras', which carve through the landscape, providing varied natural corridors.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Sant Pere de Ribes offers good gravel biking conditions for much of the year. The Mediterranean climate means mild winters, while spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails for beginners in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: Yes, while many routes in the Garraf Natural Park are challenging, there are 5 easy routes available. These often traverse the gentler agricultural plains and vineyards, offering a more relaxed experience for those new to gravel biking.

Q: Are there challenging gravel routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The Garraf Natural Park is known for its demanding terrain, with 109 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359656901/the-heart-of-the-garraf-park-in-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The heart of the Garraf park in gravel&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates through varied limestone formations and pine forests.

Q: Can I find circular gravel bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes around Sant Pere de Ribes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359548977/gravel-loop-to-l-havana-xica-of-garraf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel loop to 'l'Havana Xica' of Garraf&lt;/a&gt; offers a circular journey through the diverse landscapes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I discover along the gravel bike routes?
A: You can encounter several notable attractions. Routes may pass by historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/440250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365734075/climb-to-the-garraf-viewpoint-on-gravel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to the Garraf viewpoint on gravel&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads to panoramic views over the rugged peaks and valleys of the natural park. You might also pass the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1438355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garraf Buddhist Monastery, Sakya Tashi Ling&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other gravel bikers appreciate most about the trails in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from the rugged Garraf Natural Park to the serene vineyards, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly gravel biking options in Sant Pere de Ribes?
A: While many routes are challenging, the gentler paths through the vineyards and agricultural plains can be suitable for families. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' to find options with less elevation and smoother surfaces.

Q: Is parking available near the gravel bike trails?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Sant Pere de Ribes, especially near the town center or at trailheads leading into the Garraf Natural Park. Specific parking details can often be found in individual route descriptions on komoot.

Q: How accessible are the gravel trails by public transport?
A: Sant Pere de Ribes is well-connected by public transport, particularly buses, which can bring you close to various starting points for gravel routes. Its proximity to Sitges also means good connections to wider transport networks.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the gravel bike routes?
A: Sant Pere de Ribes itself offers a variety of cafes and restaurants where you can refuel. Many routes also pass through or near smaller villages, providing opportunities for a quick stop. The region's vineyards also offer chances for wine tastings.

Q: Are permits required for gravel biking in Garraf Natural Park?
A: Generally, permits are not required for gravel biking on established trails within the Garraf Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations and stay on marked paths to protect the natural environment.

Q: Are there routes that combine different types of scenery, like coast and mountains?
A: Yes, some routes offer a fantastic blend of landscapes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359618679/on-gravel-from-the-beach-to-the-buddhist-monastery-of-garraf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On gravel from the beach to the Buddhist monastery of Garraf&lt;/a&gt; trail takes you from coastal fringes into the heart of the Garraf Natural Park, providing a diverse scenic experience.
tips: One of the many stone cabins scattered around the Garraf, this restored stone hut is known as the "Caseta de Vinya". It has a circular plan, covered with a false dome and faces southwest. It is located a few meters from the old road from Vallgrassa to Plana Novella.
Trail that runs the Vallgrasa, about 2-3 km very easy

The Parc del Garraf astronomical observatory was born in 1997, thanks to an agreement between the Barcelona Provincial Council and the non-profit organization OAG. For this purpose, the old Can Tòfol agricultural warehouse, in the municipality of Olivella, was recovered and fitted out. These facilities are located next to the Can Grau Nature School.

The ground floor consists of a small room for conferences and activities, an exhibition hall, a digital planetarium and on the upper floor is the telescope, with a 280 mm aperture.

You can consult the public activities scheduled here: https://planetari.cat/pagines/garraf/garraf-public

Olivella is a small town located in the Garraf region, in the province of Barcelona. The municipality is made up of the small original nucleus and five urbanizations that surround it (Can Surià, la Plana Novella, les Colines, Mas Mestre and Mas Milà).

There is news of Olivella since the year 999, although it was not located in the current location. It was not until the 14th century, due to demographic growth, that the parish of Sant Pere i Sant Feliu was moved to the so-called Castell Nou, this being the starting point of the current location of the nucleus.

The Can Suriol lime kiln is located in the municipality of Olivella, in the Garraf region (Barcelona). It is a circular structure that takes advantage of the slope of the mountain as one of the walls.

These types of ovens were rented to groups of calciners who were in charge of extracting the stone, firing it and later selling it as is or in the form of lime. The process could take between a month and a month and a half. This oven operated between approximately 1930 and 1940.
